'''Walter "Walt" Simonson''' (born [[September 2]], [[1940s|1946]]) is a comic book creator who was a regular penciller, writer, and cover artist for the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] series of comics. Simonson's early professional work was for [[Wikipedia:DC Comics|DC Comics]], illustrating war stories in the early 1970s. Soon after, he and writer [[Archie Goodwin]] co-created the critically acclaimed [[Wikipedia:Manhunter (comics)|''Manhunter'']] backup feature in [[Wikipedia:Detective Comics|''Detective Comics'']].

Before he became the regular penciller on [[Star Wars]], he worked on a single issue with Goodwin and they co-created the fan favorite character, [[Beilert Valance/Legends|Beilert Valance]] in [[Star Wars (1977) 16|''The Hunter'']]. In [[1981]] he took over the pencilling chores on a regular basis, and this time working with writer [[David Michelinie]] developed such characters as [[Lumiya|Shira Brie]] and [[Plif/Legends|Plif]] and concepts like the [[talking bomb]].

After leaving ''Star Wars'', he began writing and illustrating [[Wikipedia:The Mighty Thor|''The Mighty Thor'']] series for [[Marvel Comics]], which is probably Simonson's most notable work. He also worked with his wife and writer [[Louise Simonson]] on the hit Marvel series [[Wikipedia:X-Factor|''X-Factor'']].

The character [[Wald (pilot)|Wald]], created by Simonson for [[Star Wars (1977) 60|''Marvel Star Wars #60'']], is remarkably similar to him.{{Mediacat|imagecat=Images by Walt Simonson}}
